rulings.Case Title: ALYSSA MUELLER VS. ELITE INVESTMENT & CAPITAL GROUP INC. A CALIFORNIA
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT ANDREW YATES DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED, in part, and OVERRULED, in part. No opposition filed. Defendant's demurrer to the Negligence and NIED causes of action are overruled. Defendant failed to address Plaintiff's claim that the HOA Bylaws impose a duty on Defendant not to create a nuisance or to interfere with the quiet enjoyment of another resident of the HOA or building. Defendant's demurrer to the IIED claims is overruled. Plaintiff alleges Defendant made false claims to Plaintiff's landlord that Plaintiff was using drugs and intentionally starting fires, all with the intent to drive Plaintiff out of her tenancy. Taking the allegations as true, a reasonable jury could find this type conduct constitutes outrageous behavior. Defendant's demurrer to the 11th cause of action for Tenant Harassment is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's allegations do not support an agency relationship, and the Court is not persuaded that Plaintiff can allege one based on the allegations in the TAC. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MARK SMITH VS. JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT JONES MEMORIAL HOMES INC, ARLENE HENRY DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. The Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first Cause of Action for Breach of Implied Warranty of Habitability. This is an affirmative defense to an unlawful detainer action. The demurrer is overruled on the seventh cause of action for untenantable dwelling, as the Plaintiff alleges facts that show Defendant's noncompliance with Cal. Civ. Code section 1941.1. The Demurrer is overruled as to the eighth cause of action as Plaintiff alleges sufficient facts to constitute a breach of contract. The demurrer to the UCL cause of action is overruled. The Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on the remaining causes of action for plaintiff to allege facts that show the required elements of each cause of action have been met. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: ADA LI VS. YONGXIN FENG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 8, 2022 line 13. DEFENDANT YONGXIN FENG DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. SFRO 37.9(n) is preempted by state law (C.C.P. 1179.05(a)(1)). The complaint is not otherwise uncertain; it clearly alleges a verbal agreement between Plaintiff and Defendant and Defendant's wife. Plaintiff provides sufficient information on the rent term of the lease, and the requested damages are reasonable. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
